project.name: book-rental-backend
project.description: Node.js/Express backend and Shopify frontend for managing book rentals, returns, and warehouse workflows. Integrates with Shopify Admin API and Supabase.

purpose: Provide onboarding and repository context for automated assistants (Copilot) so new instances can operate without manual orientation.

important_files.server: server.js - Express server bootstrap and route mounting
important_files.handlers: src/utils/handlers.js - Main business logic (rentals, returns, webhooks, warehouse)
important_files.frontend: frontend/*.js - Shopify theme JS for rental cart, product rental, return interface, and warehouse UI
important_files.docs: docs/*.md - Project docs, deployment guides, and frontend update logs
important_files.tests: testing/*.js - Basic test scripts (not a full test suite)

onboarding_instructions.goal: Enable Copilot to help maintain and extend the project with minimal manual context transfer.
top_tasks: Run local server and ensure env vars are configured
Review src/utils/handlers.js for endpoints and critical logic
Audit frontend files for hardcoded secrets and endpoint URLs
Run tests in testing/ and expand coverage
how_to_run_locally: Copy .env.example to .env and fill required values (SUPABASE, SHOPIFY API tokens, etc.)
Start dev server: npm run dev
For reproducible CI installs use npm ci
Note: Node.js 18+ is recommended. Use nvm or other version manager in dev environments.
onboarding_instructions.safety_and_secrets: Never store secrets in the repo. Use .env files locally and secret managers in CI. If a secret is found in code, raise it as a high-priority security issue and rotate credentials immediately.
onboarding_instructions.cross_platform_notes: Package scripts assume POSIX env-var syntax in one script; prefer cross-env for cross-platform compatibility. Windows PowerShell requires a different syntax for inline environment variables.

coding_conventions.style: Prefer consistent 2-space indentation. Preserve existing style unless changing a whole file. Keep functions modular and small. Prefer async/await for asynchronous code.
coding_conventions.error_handling: Handlers should validate input, return clear HTTP status codes, and log useful diagnostic info. External API calls should be wrapped in try/catch and fail gracefully.
coding_conventions.logging: Use console.debug/info/warn/error consistently. Include contextual identifiers in logs (request ids, order ids). Avoid logging secrets.

notable_tech_debt: Hardcoded frontend API URLs (should be configurable via theme settings or a single config file).
Frontend contains hardcoded passwords (e.g., warehouse password) — move to backend auth or secure vault.
Some deprecated package references in lockfile (review and update).
Tests are script-based; convert to a structured test framework (Jest/Mocha) and add CI integration.

for_new_copilot_instances: When asked to modify code, first open manifest.json to get context and constraints.
Prefer small, test-backed changes. Run npm ci and the test scripts after edits whenever possible.
Follow coding_conventions and update docs/readme.md when adding new scripts or env vars.

contacts_and_notes.maintainer: See repo owner: book-bugs (use repo issues or contact owner externally).
contacts_and_notes.docs_location: docs/readme.md contains developer-facing instructions and API docs. Keep it updated.
